decks.notes: The primary focus of this deck is to control the opponent through counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By utilizing cards like Force of Will, Force of Negation, Swords to Plowshares, and Prismatic Ending, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain board control. The addition of Planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student adds another layer of card advantage and control. With a mix of counterspells, removal, and card draw, this UWx Control deck is designed to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in a Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact-based combos with cards like Goblin Welder, Emry, Lurker of the Loch, and Pinnacle Emissary to generate value and win the game. Urza's Saga is a key land in the deck, allowing for tutoring and artifact creation. The deck also includes cards like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal to ramp and get ahead on resources. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain provides additional utility and card advantage, while cards like Skateboard and Agatha's Soul Cauldron offer alternative win conditions. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with powerful artifact synergies.

The deck focuses on utilizing the powerful combo card Doomsday to set up a winning sequence of cards that lead to victory. The deck includes various cards that can help search for and set up the Doomsday combo, such as Thoughtseize, Brainstorm, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. It also includes protection cards like Force of Will and Daze to protect the combo from being disrupted. Additionally, the deck includes acceleration cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to help cast Doomsday quickly and efficiently.

The deck revolves around quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creature targets and using various spells to bring them back into play for a game-winning swing. The goal is to efficiently combo off by utilizing cards like Reanimate, Archon of Cruelty, and Animate Dead to bring creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier back into play. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Cabal Therapy and Unmask to disrupt opponents' plans. Overall, it aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful reanimated creatures.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with Gaea's Cradle and creatures like Badgermole Cub and Ignoble Hierarch. It uses control elements like Wasteland and Reclamation Sage to disrupt the opponent's game plan while searching for key creatures with Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Craterhoof Behemoth and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to overwhelm the opponent. By efficiently managing resources and controlling the board, this deck aims to establish dominance and secure victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The main idea of this Legacy Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By using cards like Lightning Bolt, Lava Spike, and Chain Lightning, it aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Bauble provide card advantage and help with deck manipulation. The deck also features creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Slickshot Show-Off, and Dragon's Rage Channeler to apply pressure on the opponent. The mana base includes Wooded Foothills, Wasteland, and Scalding Tarn to ensure consistency in casting spells.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, then using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back into play for a game-ending attack. The cards Shallow Grave and Faithless Looting help facilitate this strategy, while Thoughtseize and Unmask can disrupt opponents' plans. Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers and Collective Brutality provide additional versatility and options for controlling the game. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently reanimate big threats and overwhelm the opponent before they can respond effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to generate aggressive tempo plays through efficient spellcasting and creature attacks. By utilizing cards like Daze, Force of Will, and Lightning Bolt to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also deploying threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Cori-Steel Cutter to pressure the opponent's life total, this deck aims to outpace and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive strategy with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands for consistent color fixing and access to the spells needed to maintain the aggressive tempo.

The deck focuses on using land-based control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while assembling a powerful combo involving Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to create a 20/20 indestructible Marit Lage token. The deck includes various utility lands like The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale and Maze of Ith to further control the board, as well as cards like Exploration and Life from the Loam to generate value and card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through strategic land usage and control elements.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources through a combination of artifacts and control spells. It utilizes cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere to slow down the opponent, while also including cards like Urza's Saga and Urza's Workshop to create a powerful board presence. The deck aims to establish a prison-like strategy to control the game and eventually overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The key concept is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up a strong board presence to quickly close out the game.

The main focus of this deck is to lock down opponents by utilizing artifacts that disrupt their game plan and slow down their actions. By using cards like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to control the board and prevent opponents from effectively executing their strategies. This deck also includes powerful artifacts like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and The One Ring to provide additional control and threats on the battlefield. Overall, the deck aims to establish a strong prison-like lock on the game through the use of artifact control pieces.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of countermagic, removal, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with disruptive spells like Stifle and Force of Will, while also utilizing efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also leverages card selection and filtering with Brainstorm, Ponder, and cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Overall, the deck aims to out-value the opponent by generating card advantage and controlling the board through a combination of removal and permission spells.

The deck revolves around cheating powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Griselbrand onto the battlefield by using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The goal is to quickly put these threats into play before the opponent can react, utilizing mana acceleration from cards like Lotus Petal and fast lands like City of Traitors and Ancient Tomb. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo. The key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with big, game-ending threats early in the game.

The focus of this deck is to establish control over the game utilizing artifact cards and overwhelming the opponent with powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. The deck uses cards like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while using cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Urza's Saga to search for key artifacts. The inclusion of cards like Manifold Key and Voltaic Key allow for additional utility and flexibility in using artifacts to gain advantage and secure victory.

This deck aims to lock down the opponent by using artifacts to control the game. Cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere help to disrupt the opponent's plays, while cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith provide mana acceleration. The win condition is to assemble a powerful board state with cards like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, while also utilizing cards such as Planar Nexus and The One Ring for additional control and advantage. Overall, the deck focuses on resource denial and overwhelming the opponent with powerful artifacts.

The main concept of this Grixis Aggro deck is to apply early pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler, backed up by disruption spells like Daze, Force of Will, and Thoughtseize. The deck also includes card draw and deck filtering spells such as Ponder and Brainstorm to help maintain consistency. The mana base is optimized for maximum efficiency, using fetchlands like Polluted Delta and Bloodstained Mire to find the necessary dual lands. Overall, the deck aims to control the board and close out the game quickly with its aggressive threats.
